title: The Imperial Truth
notes: Collection/anthology row present; individual contents not explicitly recoverable from TLBranson list.

curation.synopsis: Six short stories drawn from across the early decades of the Great Crusade and the first treacheries of the Heresy, gathered from a group of established Black Library voices and edited by Laurie Goulding. Rob Sanders opens with a Custodian errand carried out at the Emperor's own command. Gav Thorpe rides with the Dark Angels under their silent Primarch. Aaron Dembski-Bowden returns Angron to the red sands of Nuceria. James Swallow weighs what is left when a Legion is broken. Nick Kyme follows the Phoenician at the edge of his fall, and Graham McNeill closes with a hidden adoratrice from the early Imperial cult. A sampler that ranges across Legions and decades and never settles long in one set of colours. Originally a 2013 Horus Heresy Weekender exclusive of two thousand numbered hardbacks, later released digitally and folded into the War Without End anthology.

flags.reason: Roster lists 'collection' but the volume gathers stories from six different authors (Sanders, Thorpe, Dembski-Bowden, Swallow, Kyme, McNeill) under editor L.J. Goulding; multi-author short-story compilations are conventionally anthologies.
Six independent stories with distinct casts; only the most prominent named POV/figures are tagged at the volume level.
